title: US forces enter Iran by..?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify, however intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: US forces enter Iran by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify; however, intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Note: Only US military personnel who deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran for operational purposes (e.g., military, humanitarian, etc.) will qualify. Pilots who are shot down, or other cases in which US military personnel do not deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran, will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: US special forces conducted confirmed ground insertions into Iran this week to rescue downed Air Force pilots, marking the first verified US troop entries amid the ongoing air campaign launched February 28 alongside Israel, which has struck over 2,000 targets including nuclear sites and the oil hub at Kharg Island. President Trump issued fresh ultimatums demanding Iran reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face infrastructure attacks, as 50,000 US troops reinforce the region with marines, A-10 close air support, and amphibious assets signaling potential full-scale invasion. Iranian missile barrages on US bases have killed at least 13 Americans, heightening escalation risks ahead of any ceasefire talks or further deadlines.

title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another, or for an official ceasefire agreement to be otherwise confirmed to have been reached by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's April 8 announcement of a two-week ceasefire, with Israel halting strikes on Iran amid negotiations, marks the freshest diplomatic signal in the US-Iran war that began February 28, 2026, as Pakistani Premier Shehbaz Sharif declared the truce effective immediately and extending to allies like Lebanon. However, Iran rejected a 45-day ceasefire proposal hours prior, insisting on a permanent end to hostilities while refusing to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, prompting Trump's looming ultimatum for strikes on power plants and infrastructure. Pakistan's mediation via the proposed "Islamabad Accord"—trading nuclear limits for sanctions relief—offers a potential breakthrough, but ongoing US airstrikes and mutual rejections underscore high escalation risks ahead of the deadline.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain edges trader consensus at 16% implied probability for the 2026 FIFA World Cup title after capping a perfect UEFA qualifying campaign unbeaten and posting solid March friendlies—a 3-0 win over Serbia and scoreless draw versus Egypt—extending their competitive streak from Euro 2024 glory. France climbed to 13.5% with international break triumphs over Brazil and Colombia, boosting confidence in their stacked squad despite occasional cohesion issues, while England's upset loss to Japan nudged them to 11.5%. Argentina (9.2%) and Brazil (8.6%) remain close contenders via CONMEBOL table dominance, fostering tight odds amid deep talent pools, navigable group draws, and the 48-team format's volatility ahead of June kickoff.

title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices California Governor Gavin Newsom as the clear early frontrunner for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 25.9%, reflecting his consistent leads in recent primary polls, including a commanding edge over Kamala Harris in California (March 12) and a tie for second in New Hampshire (March 27). Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8.6% buoyed by progressive grassroots energy and fundraising prowess, while Jon Ossoff's 5.8% stems from his Georgia swing-state incumbency amid 2026 midterm pressures. The fragmented field underscores a wide-open primary with no declared candidates; consolidation could hinge on midterm results elevating battleground performers like Mark Kelly or Gretchen Whitmer, major fundraising announcements, or party endorsements post-November elections.

title: The Masters - Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler's world No. 1 ranking and two prior green jackets anchor his 13.5% trader consensus as Masters favorite, bolstered by elite Augusta history, but recent family commitments following his second child's birth introduce uncertainty about peak sharpness. Jon Rahm's LIV Golf dominance—one win, three runner-ups—elevates him to 7.8%, while Rory McIlroy's defending champion status at 6.5% faces historical repeat hurdles, matched by Bryson DeChambeau's power-suited form at the same probability. Xander Schauffele and Ludvig Aberg trail closely amid a deep field of 22 debutants and recovering Collin Morikawa post-back injury, with dry, breezy Augusta conditions favoring precision players and keeping the race tightly contested.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Israel strikes continue targeting Iranian petrochemical complexes, bridges, and military sites on Kharg Island, while Iran maintains missile barrages toward Israel and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, over a month into the conflict that began with February 28 airstrikes assassinating Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. President Trump extended a deadline for Iran to halt shipping attacks before announcing a potential two-week ceasefire amid heated negotiations, though Tehran rejected shorter proposals demanding a permanent end and vows retaliation. Traders monitor fragile diplomacy, mediator efforts like Pakistan's involvement, and escalation risks from energy infrastructure hits, with no immediate resolution in sight despite de-escalation signals.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (LOW) $30 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil prices have surged past $112 per barrel in early April 2026, reaching the highest levels since June 2022, primarily driven by escalating geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz that have embedded a substantial risk premium into trader consensus. U.S. inventories provide a counterbalance, climbing 5.5 million barrels to 461.6 million in the week ended March 27—the highest since mid-2023—signaling ample supply amid OPEC+ gradual production increases. The EIA's Short-Term Energy Outlook projects Brent peaking near $115/bbl in Q2 2026 on steady global demand growth, though macroeconomic factors like interest rates and China imports loom large. Traders eye weekly EIA inventory reports and the upcoming IEA Oil Market Report for volatility catalysts through month-end.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim edge over California Governor Gavin Newsom in trader consensus for the 2028 presidential winner, with odds reflecting Republican momentum from the 2024 Electoral College victory and Vance's strong positioning in early GOP primary polls, such as his lead in New Hampshire surveys from February. Newsom's rise stems from a March California primary poll showing him trouncing Kamala Harris, bolstering his Democratic frontrunner status amid party fragmentation post-defeat. The race remains tightly contested due to the early stage—over two years before primaries—with no formal campaigns, fragmented fields on both sides, and high uncertainty from potential 2026 midterm shifts, cabinet successes, scandals, or endorsements that could widen gaps.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President J.D. Vance and HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. lead trader consensus at 38% and 49% implied probabilities for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, driven by their high-profile roles in President Trump's second-term administration amid constitutional term limits preventing his reelection. RFK Jr.'s Make America Healthy Again (MAHA) agenda has mobilized independents and health-focused voters as a midterm wild card, though recent Republican Senate resistance and a March glyphosate executive order have sparked backlash. Vance holds strong MAGA base support as heir apparent, while Marco Rubio's odds surged to 22% following reports Trump polled major donors preferring him over Vance. Approaching 2026 midterms and primary positioning will test these dynamics.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' dominant 2026 chassis and power unit have propelled teammates George Russell and Kimi Antonelli to the top of trader consensus for the Drivers' Championship, with Russell's 43.5% implied probability edging Antonelli's 30.8% despite the Italian rookie's nine-point standings lead (72-63) after podium sweeps in Australia, China, and Japan. Antonelli's recent Suzuka victory—via a timely safety car—marked him as the youngest-ever championship leader, but his admitted start struggles and qualifying inconsistencies contrast Russell's consistent podiums marred only by a battery failure there. Ferrari duo Charles Leclerc (49 points) and Lewis Hamilton trail amid a 45-point constructors' deficit, while others like Lando Norris and Max Verstappen lag on inferior machinery, emphasizing Mercedes' early-season edge ahead of Miami.

eventMetadata.context_description: Boston Celtics (53-25, second in Eastern Conference) welcome the Charlotte Hornets (43-36, eighth in East) to TD Garden with a rare clean injury report, confirming full availability of stars Jayson Tatum, Jaylen Brown, and Derrick White after prior load management and minor ailments. This development bolsters trader consensus favoring the hosts amid Boston's three-game win streak, elite home-court advantage (26-11), and 7-1 dominance in recent head-to-heads. Hornets counter with momentum from four straight wins, including a 122-108 upset over Minnesota, but face road woes against top foes as play-in tournament positioning looms. Late-season rest edges and defensive matchups on LaMelo Ball could sway the outcome.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's league-best 62-16 record and status as defending 2025 NBA champions, led by MVP frontrunner Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, anchor their 39.5% implied probability as traders price in their Western Conference dominance and playoff home-court edge through the regular season finale on April 12. San Antonio Spurs' explosive 27-3 surge since February, fueled by Victor Wembanyama's elite two-way play and top-three defensive/offensive rankings, elevates them to 15.7%, positioning them as the primary West threat with a 59-19 mark. Boston Celtics' steady 53-25 Eastern Conference standing sustains 11.3% odds amid Detroit Pistons' surprising No. 1 East seed at 57-22, while Denver Nuggets hold 10.5% on Nikola Jokić's triple-double averages despite tighter seeding battles; Luka Dončić's hamstring injury craters Lakers' longshot chances.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chicago Bulls (29-49, 12th in East) face the Washington Wizards (17-61, 15th in East) in a late-season clash with no playoff implications, both teams prioritizing draft lottery positioning amid heavy injury tolls. Bulls enter on a seven-game skid, ruling out Josh Giddey (hamstring), Matas Buzelis (illness), Anfernee Simons (wrist fracture), and several season-enders like Jalen Smith (calf), while Collin Sexton (finger) and Lachlan Olbrich (plantar fasciitis) are probable. Wizards list 10 players on report, including Justin Champagnie (out), Tristan Vukcevic (knee), and Alex Sarr (questionable), relying on rookies like Will Riley amid roster instability. Depleted lineups and rest/load management heighten upset potential in this matchup at Capital One Arena.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by March 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US airstrikes targeted military sites on Kharg Island overnight on April 7, sparing critical oil infrastructure that handles 90% of Iran's crude exports, as confirmed by US officials amid President Trump's deadline for Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian state media asserts the island remains fully under armed forces control, with exports uninterrupted and defenses reinforced since late March against seizure threats. No ground operations or occupation have materialized, sustaining trader consensus on persistent Iranian sovereignty despite escalation risks from potential further strikes, naval blockades, or diplomatic breakthroughs. Upcoming Hormuz negotiations or retaliatory actions could tip control dynamics in this Persian Gulf flashpoint.

title: Jazz vs. Pelicans
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 7 at 8:00PM ET:
If the Jazz win, the market will resolve to "Jazz".
If the Pelicans win, the market will resolve to "Pelicans".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Both Western Conference bottom-feeders, the Utah Jazz (21-58) and New Orleans Pelicans (25-54), enter their April 7 matchup at Smoothie King Center with playoff hopes long extinguished, prioritizing draft lottery positioning amid heavy injury reports and load management. Pelicans rule out Trey Murphy III (ankle sprain), Dejounte Murray (hand contusion), and Bryce McGowens (toe fracture), with Karlo Matkovic questionable (back spasms); Jazz list Elijah Harkless (hamstring) out, Ace Bailey doubtful (knee), alongside Keyonte George (ankle) and Walker Kessler (shoulder) unavailable. New Orleans swept a February back-to-back (129-118, 115-105) despite similar absences, holding home-court edge and slightly better recent form (2-8 last 10 vs. Jazz 1-9), shaping trader consensus around a low-stakes, unpredictable contest.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Minnesota Timberwolves (46-32) enter their road matchup against the injury-decimated Indiana Pacers (18-60) hampered by the absence of star guard Anthony Edwards (right knee) and forward Jaden McDaniels (knee), per the official NBA injury report released April 7, fueling a recent skid that includes a 122-108 loss to Charlotte on April 5. Pacers counter with nine players listed, including Tyrese Haliburton (Achilles out) and Johnny Furphy (ACL, season-ending), amid their league-worst record and 0-10 stretch in last 10 games. Despite mutual absences, trader consensus favors Minnesota's superior talent, depth, and Western Conference playoff seeding incentives over Indiana's tank-mode roster, though short-handed contests heighten upset potential.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

markets.question: Will there be no change in Fed interest rates after the April 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors no change at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, with a 97.4% implied probability for maintaining the federal funds rate at 3.50%-3.75%, reflecting the resilience of recent U.S. economic data amid heightened geopolitical risks. The robust March nonfarm payrolls report, adding 178,000 jobs—well above the 59,000 consensus—coupled with unemployment dipping to 4.3%, underscores a firm labor market that tempers rate-cut expectations following the Fed's March hold and single-cut dot-plot projection for 2026. Elevated oil prices above $110 per barrel from Iran-related tensions further dampen easing bets. Realistic challenges include a hotter-than-expected March CPI release on April 10, potentially lifting the slim 1.1% hike odds, though downside risks for cuts remain negligible absent abrupt weakness.

title: Colombia Chamber of Representatives Election: 3rd Place
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Official results from Colombia's March 8, 2026, parliamentary election, certified by the Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil, position the Partido Liberal Colombiano (PLC) with 26 seats in the 188-member Chamber of Representatives—third behind the Historic Pact's 42 seats and Centro Democrático's 30—driving trader consensus to 96% implied probability for PLC in third place under the proportional representation system across multi-member departmental constituencies. The PLC outperformed traditional rivals like the Partido Conservador Colombiano (19 seats) and Partido de la U amid a fragmented congress requiring coalitions, reflecting voter shifts toward ideological blocs in low-turnout voting marred by pre-election violence. Scenarios challenging this include rare seat reallocations from ongoing departmental recounts or legal disputes, though no major challenges have emerged in the past month.

title: Kansas City Royals vs. Cleveland Guardians
description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Kansas City Royals and Cleveland Guardians, scheduled for April 7 at 6:10PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Kansas City Royals" if the Kansas City Royals win the game.

This market will resolve to "Cleveland Guardians" if the Cleveland Guardians win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will there be a run scored in the first inning?: Kansas City Royals vs. Cleveland Guardians
markets.description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Kansas City Royals and Cleveland Guardians, scheduled for April 7 at 6:10 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes Run" if at least one run is scored in the 1st inning by either the Kansas City Royals or Cleveland Guardians.

This market will resolve to "No Run" if no runs are scored in the 1st inning by either team.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: The rubber match of this AL Central series at Progressive Field sees Kansas City Royals ace Cole Ragans on the mound for the visitors, following a tight 1-1 split with the Royals' 4-2 win behind Michael Wacha's seven scoreless innings on April 6 and Cleveland's 2-1 walk-off victory via Brayan Rocchio's single on April 7 behind Gavin Williams. Both teams sit around 5-6 early in the standings, with dominant pitching driving low-scoring outcomes amid frigid Cleveland weather that shifted Tuesday's start time. Guardians shortstop Gabriel Arias exited Monday with hamstring tightness (10-day IL likely), while Royals relievers Carlos Estévez (foot) and Bailey Falter (elbow) remain sidelined, thinning late-inning options in a matchup favoring strong starters and home-field resilience.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 8?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Milwaukee Bucks (31-47), clinging to 11th in the Eastern Conference, face the league-worst Brooklyn Nets (19-59) in a late-season matchup at Barclays Center, with both teams long eliminated from playoffs and focused on draft lottery positioning. Bucks trader sentiment hinges on a devastating injury report listing Giannis Antetokounmpo (left knee hyperextension/bone bruise), Bobby Portis (wrist), Gary Trent Jr. (hip), Kyle Kuzma (Achilles), and others as out, exacerbating their 1-7 SU road skid in the last eight away games. Nets counter with nine players sidelined, including Nic Claxton (finger), but boast recent home momentum after a 121-115 win over Washington and a 127-82 blowout of Milwaukee in December. Minimal rest advantages and tanking incentives keep probabilities closely contested.

title: Lightning vs. Senators
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 7 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Lightning win, the market will resolve to "Lightning".
If the Senators win, the market will resolve to "Senators".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tampa Bay Lightning head to Ottawa Senators for a pivotal late-season Atlantic Division matchup with playoff seeding on the line, as the Lightning (48-23-6) hold first place while Senators (40-27-10) vie for a wild-card spot. Both squads face significant injury challenges: Ottawa's blue line remains depleted without Thomas Chabot (recently practicing post-arm injury), Tyler Kleven, Carter Yakemchuk, Dennis Gilbert, and Nick Jensen, leaning heavily on Jake Sanderson-Artem Zub pairing and Linus Ullmark in net. Lightning counter absences of Anthony Cirelli, Brandon Hagel, Pontus Holmberg (fresh upper-body hit into boards), and others, scratching Victor Hedman while starting Jonas Johansson to rest Andrei Vasilevskiy. Tampa's superior depth and road form tilt trader consensus despite mutual vulnerabilities.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 97% for the Iranian regime falling by April 30, driven by its resilience despite the Supreme Leader Khamenei's March assassination, quelled January protests that killed thousands, and ongoing US-Israeli airstrikes degrading military infrastructure. US intelligence assessments in early March affirmed no imminent collapse risk, citing loyal security forces, severe internal controls, and absent mass defections or coordinated uprisings—key historical indicators of regime failure. Recent de-escalation via a two-week ceasefire announced April 7, amid Strait of Hormuz tensions, further bolsters stability. Realistic shifts would require elite betrayals, widespread defections, or a decisive military rout before month-end, though external pressure alone rarely triggers rapid overthrow without internal opposition.

eventMetadata.context_description: Edmonton Oilers head to Delta Center as heavy underdogs against the Utah Mammoth in a pivotal late-season inter-conference clash, with both squads battling for playoff berths—Oilers at 39-29-9 holding a Pacific wild-card spot and Utah at 40-30-6 vying in the Central Division. Edmonton's trader consensus reflects major absences: Leon Draisaitl (lower-body IR, return ~April 18) and Zach Hyman (undisclosed, out through road trip), forcing Connor McDavid to anchor a depleted attack post a 5-1 loss to Vegas that snapped their five-game win streak. Utah rides momentum from Clayton Keller's hat trick in a 7-4 rout of Vancouver, bolstered by home-ice edge despite missing Barrett Hayton and Jack McBain (both out); watch for MacKenzie Weegar's day-to-day status and goaltending duel between Trent Jarry and Karel Vejmelka, following Edmonton's 5-2 victory in their March 24 matchup.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Oklahoma City Thunder, locked in as the Western Conference's No. 1 seed at 62-16, head to Los Angeles as heavy favorites against a battered Lakers squad fighting for playoff positioning at 50-28. Critical injury blows define trader sentiment: LeBron James was ruled out hours before tipoff with left foot injury management—his eighth absence in recent games—joining Luka Dončić (left hamstring strain) and Austin Reaves (oblique, out for the regular season) on the sidelines, forcing a makeshift lineup of Luke Kennard, Jake LaRavia, Rui Hachimura, Jarred Vanderbilt, and Deandre Ayton. OKC, 3-0 against LA this year including a 139-96 rout on April 2, boasts near-full health aside from Alex Caruso's questionable illness status, with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander leading their elite form into this late-season clash.

title: Baltimore Orioles vs. Chicago White Sox
description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Baltimore Orioles and Chicago White Sox, scheduled for April 7 at 7:40PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Baltimore Orioles" if the Baltimore Orioles win the game.

This market will resolve to "Chicago White Sox" if the Chicago White Sox win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Baltimore Orioles head to Chicago seeking a series sweep over the White Sox after 2-1 and 4-2 victories in the first two games, fueled by spot pitching gems and Gunnar Henderson's back-to-back homers amid a snapped three-game skid. Kyle Bradish (0-2, 6.23 ERA) starts for Baltimore against Sean Burke (0-1, 3.60 ERA) for host Chicago, whose staff has surrendered 6.56 runs per game early. White Sox outfielder Austin Hays landed on the 10-day IL with a hamstring strain post-Monday loss, thinning their lineup further. Both clubs hover around .400 winning percentage in the young AL East and Central races, with cold Guaranteed Rate Field conditions potentially favoring pitchers.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's duo Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen lead trader consensus for the Eurovision Song Contest 2026 winner at 38.6% implied probability following their landslide victory at national final UMK on February 28, where "Liekinheitin" amassed record public votes for its violin-pop fusion and high-energy staging, propelling early frontrunner momentum validated by predictive models like The Model. Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund holds second at 10.3% after recent odds climbs overtaking France, buoyed by strong melodic appeal and historical Nordic televote strength, while France's operatic powerhouse Monroe sits at 10.0% on consistent artistic pedigree—highlighted by her April 7 cover of Finland's entry sparking cross-border buzz. With more national finals pending and Vienna semifinals approaching, jury-televote splits and staging rehearsals remain key swing factors in this fluid contest.

title: Heat vs. Raptors
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 7 at 7:30PM ET:
If the Heat win, the market will resolve to "Heat".
If the Raptors win, the market will resolve to "Raptors".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the Eastern Conference playoff picture tightly contested—Raptors clinging to the No. 6 seed at 43-35 while Heat battle from 10th, just 3.5 games back—this road matchup carries high stakes for seeding and play-in avoidance. Heat enter shorthanded, ruling out Nikola Jovic (left ankle sprain) and Terry Rozier (not with team), though Tyler Herro (foot) and returning Norman Powell (illness) are probable, tilting usage toward Bam Adebayo and Andrew Wiggins. Raptors near full strength after upgrading Immanuel Quickley (plantar fasciitis), Collin Murray-Boyles (quad), and Sandro Mamukelashvili (knee) to available, bolstering their starting five of Quickley, RJ Barrett, Scottie Barnes, Brandon Ingram, and Jakob Poeltl. Toronto's home-court edge and prior 112-91 rout of Miami underscore trader focus on defensive matchups and momentum in this back-loaded schedule stretch.

title: Trump announces end of military operations against Iran by ...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President Trump, the US government, or the military publicly and officially announce that their military operations against Iran, initiated on February 28, 2026, have concluded by the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Qualifying statements must clearly indicate that the operation has ended. Informal announcements, statements from unnamed sources or leaks will not qualify. 

Written public statements from Donald Trump (e.g. posts from his personal Truth Social account), will count. Videos posted on his social media accounts will also qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the US government and/or its official representatives; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Qualifying statements must clearly indicate that the operation has ended. Informal announcements, statements from unnamed sources or leaks will not qualify. 

Written public statements from Donald Trump (e.g. posts from his personal Truth Social account), will count. Videos posted on his social media accounts will also qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the US government and/or its official representatives; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Trump announces end of military operations against Iran by May 31st?

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's announcement hours ago suspending the US bombing campaign against Iran and agreeing to a two-week ceasefire—issued just before his self-imposed deadline to target key infrastructure like Kharg Island—marks the most significant de-escalation signal since joint US-Israel strikes began on February 28, 2026, targeting Iran's nuclear facilities, missile sites, and military assets amid Strait of Hormuz tensions. This follows recent US airstrikes, a downed pilot rescue, and repeated ultimatums, with Vice President JD Vance stating military objectives are largely met. Traders weigh the ceasefire's expiration as a potential trigger for an end-of-operations declaration, alongside ongoing diplomacy, Iranian retaliation risks, and oil market volatility, against historical patterns of prolonged Middle East conflicts. Upcoming negotiations or renewed escalation could shift timelines.
